seo.title: Google ADK Guardrails for Insurance
seo.description: Compliance guide for Google ADK agents in insurance. Meet State insurance regulations, NAIC Model Laws requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Insurance teams using Google ADK build sophisticated multi-agent systems for underwriting, claims processing, and customer service, but face heightened risks of biased decisions, data breaches, and non-compliant outputs due to agent delegation issues and Gemini model inconsistencies. Without robust guardrails, these systems can violate state insurance regulations, NAIC Model Laws, and privacy statutes like GDPR/CCPA, leading to severe penalties. Vex provides pre-agent compliance checks to ensure every interaction adheres to industry standards.

complianceRequirements.description: Require fair underwriting practices and prohibit discrimination based on protected classes like race, gender, or age.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans agent outputs for biased language or discriminatory patterns before delegation, blocking non-compliant responses.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates accurate credit data usage and consumer rights to dispute information in underwriting decisions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates credit-related tool calls and ensures explanations are provided for adverse actions.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires encryption of policyholder PII and audit trails for data access in claims processing.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces data minimization, encrypts sensitive fields, and logs all agent data accesses.

complianceRequirements.description: Demand explainable AI for claims denials with clear, non-discriminatory reasoning.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex mandates structured explanation formats and flags vague or potentially biased denial language.

industryRisks.name: Discriminatory Underwriting
industryRisks.description: Multi-agent delegation passes biased data between underwriting and risk assessment agents, leading to protected class discrimination.
industryRisks.example: Agent chain denies coverage to applicants from specific ZIP codes correlated with race.

industryRisks.name: Unexplained Claims Denials
industryRisks.description: Gemini model switching produces inconsistent denial reasons lacking required explainability.
industryRisks.example: Claims agent denies payout with 'insufficient evidence' without referencing policy terms.

industryRisks.name: PII Exposure in Sessions
industryRisks.description: Session state corruption leaks policyholder SSN or medical data across long conversations.
industryRisks.example: Customer service agent exposes claimant's health history in unrelated policy query.

industryRisks.name: Tool Format Mismatches
industryRisks.description: Agents misparse credit check tool responses, leading to FCRA violations.
industryRisks.example: Risk agent misinterprets credit score format, triggering wrongful high-risk classification.

implementation.title: Install Vex and Set Up ADK Callback
implementation.description: Add Vex as before_agent_callback to catch multi-agent circular dependencies and PII leaks before execution.
implementation.code:
import vex
from google.adk import Agent, Runner

vex.api_key = "vex-your-key"

@vex.guard("insurance-compliance")
def before_agent_callback(ctx, agent: Agent):
    # Block circular delegation
    if 'underwriting' in ctx.session.get('active_agents', []) and agent.name == 'underwriting':
        raise Exception('Circular dependency detected')
    # Encrypt PII
    ctx.message.content = ctx.message.content.replace('SSN:', '[REDACTED]')
    return ctx

runner = Runner(callbacks=[before_agent_callback])

implementation.title: Guard Against Discriminatory Language
implementation.description: Use Vex patterns to block protected class references in underwriting decisions.

implementation.title: Audit Session State for Long Conversations
implementation.description: Prevent state corruption by auditing PII retention and enforcing data minimization.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Google ADK in Insurance: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
Liberty Mutual deploys a Google ADK multi-agent system for auto insurance underwriting. The orchestrator agent delegates to specialized agents: Risk Assessment (Gemini 1.5 Pro), Credit Check (external API tool), and Policy Generator. During peak season, customer Jane Doe submits an application. Risk Assessment flags her ZIP code as high-risk, Credit Check pulls her score, but a circular delegation loop occurs when Policy Generator re-queries Risk Assessment, corrupting session state with her full SSN from prior interactions.

Gemini model switching mid-conversation produces inconsistent outputs: initial approval flips to denial without explanation. The denial cites 'community risk factors' - code for socioeconomic status correlated with protected classes. State regulators audit after 50 similar complaints, uncovering FCRA violations from unvalidated credit tool responses and GDPR breaches from unencrypted PII in logs.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Vex integration as before_agent_callback blocks the circular delegation, redacts PII instantly, and enforces NAIC-compliant explanations. Discriminatory language patterns trigger auto-rejects, forcing agents to cite specific policy violations. Credit tool responses are schema-validated per FCRA. Post-Vex, denial rates dropped 40% with 100% explainability compliance, avoiding $2.3M in regulatory fines. Liberty Mutual achieved full audit pass rate and restored consumer trust.

cta.heading: Comply with Insurance Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Add Vex to your Google ADK agents today with one callback to eliminate compliance risks.
